TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /auth 2. Set Headers 3. Add JSON Credentials 4. Send POST request
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains a valid token string

TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /auth 2. Set JSON Credentials (invalid) 3. Send POST request
Steps to Execute: Send POST to /auth with invalid credentials { "username": "bad_user", "password": "bad_pass" }
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains reason "Bad credentials"
Misc (Comments): Per API doc, failure still returns 200 but yields a reason instead of token

Steps to Execute: Send GET to /booking without any query parameters
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains an array of all booking IDs

PreCondition: A booking with specific firstname/lastname exists
TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking 2. Append query parameters firstname and lastname 3. Send GET request
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains an array of filtered booking IDs matching the name

PreCondition: A valid booking ID is known (e.g., ID=1)
TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (Accept: application/json) 3. Send GET request
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains a complete JSON object of the booking details

TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Send GET request using an ID that does not exist
1. Define Endpoint /booking 2. Set Headers (Content-Type, Accept) 3. Construct booking JSON payload 4. Send POST request
Steps to Execute: Send POST to /booking with fully populated booking JSON (firstname, lastname, totalprice, depositpaid, bookingdates, additionalneeds)
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response contains full booking details and an assigned bookingid

TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking 2. Set Headers 3. Provide empty or malformed JSON payload 4. Send POST request
Steps to Execute: Send POST to /booking with an empty request body
Expected Result: Status 500 Internal Server Error (or API equivalent failure code); booking not created

PreCondition: Booking ID exists; User possesses a valid Auth Token
TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (Content-Type, Accept, Cookie containing token) 3. Pass updated JSON payload 4. Send PUT request
Steps to Execute: Send PUT to /booking/1 including Cookie: token=<valid_token> with full updated payload
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response returns the updated booking JSON object reflecting changes

TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (without Cookie or Authorization) 3. Pass full payload 4. Send PUT request
Steps to Execute: Send PUT to /booking/1 without Auth Token cookie
Expected Result: Status 403 Forbidden; booking details are not altered

PreCondition: Booking ID exists; User possesses a valid Auth Token
TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (Content-Type, Accept, Cookie containing token) 3. Pass partial JSON payload 4. Send PATCH request
Steps to Execute: Send PATCH to /booking/1 including Cookie: token=<valid_token> overriding only firstname and lastname
Expected Result: Status 200 OK; response returns the updated booking JSON with merged details

PreCondition: Booking ID exists; User possesses a valid Auth Token
TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (Content-Type, Cookie containing token) 3. Send DELETE request
Steps to Execute: Send DELETE to /booking/1 including Cookie: token=<valid_token>
Expected Result: Status 201 Created; booking record is successfully deleted
Misc (Comments): Status 201 is the expected return code for Delete per Restful Booker documentation

TestSteps: 1. Define Endpoint /booking/{id} 2. Set Headers (without Cookie or Authorization) 3. Send DELETE request
Steps to Execute: Send DELETE to /booking/1 without Auth Token cookie
Expected Result: Status 403 Forbidden; booking is not deleted

Misc (Comments): Status 201 is the unique expected return code for Ping per the documentation
